extra.reasoning:
The only transition that can set sent=true while opted_out=true is t_resume_glitch (line 13), which fires on send_attempt only when opted_out and acc == 28. So the property is violated iff acc can reach the value 28.

Each step event's nested if-then-else folds to acc := (3*acc + c) mod 48, where c = 8 (step_0), 38 (step_1), 17 (step_2). I verified that the triple-nested subtract-48 reduction equals mod 48 for every acc in [0,47] (max raw value 3*47+38 = 179 < 4*48, so up to three subtractions suffice).

Starting from acc=0, a full BFS over the 48-value state space enumerates every reachable acc, independent of sequence length. The reachable set is exactly {0, 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44, 47} (17 values). 28 is NOT in this set.

Invariant explanation: all three constants are ≡ 2 (mod 3), 3*acc ≡ 0 (mod 3), and 48 ≡ 0 (mod 3) so the mod-48 reduction preserves residue mod 3. Hence after the first step every reachable acc ≡ 2 (mod 3). The target 28 ≡ 1 (mod 3), so it is unreachable by any sequence of step events. Since acc can never equal 28, t_resume_glitch can never fire; on send_attempt while opted_out, only t_send_blocked applies (sent:=false). The optout transition itself sets sent:=false. Therefore globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. No counterexample exists.
